title: Smoky Molten Monsoon Slovakian Maple Syrup Wrap
description: A unique Slovakian-inspired dish featuring smoky flavors and a molten maple syrup center, all wrapped in a corn husk. This keto-friendly wrap is perfect for a quick breakfast or snack.

ingredients.name: Smoked Pork Shoulder
ingredients.notes: Slow-cooked in a 2251 Fahrenheit oven for 4 hours.

ingredients.name: Keto-Friendly Seasoning
ingredients.notes: Custom blend of herbs and spices.

instructions: Soak the corn husks in water for at least 30 minutes before use.
Preheat the oven to 3751 degrees Fahrenheit.
Wrap the smoked pork shoulder in aluminum foil and bake for 2 hours.
Meanwhile, crisp-cook the bacon in a skillet until golden brown.
Chop the garlic and mix with heavy cream, cheddar cheese, and keto-friendly seasoning.
Spread a layer of the garlic mixture on one half of the corn husk.
Top with diced jalape20, chopped scallions, crumbled coleslaw mix, diced tomato, diced red onion, and diced avocado.
Drizzle with smoked paprika and lime juice.
Fold the other half of the corn husk over the filling and secure with kitchen twine.
Bake the wrap in the preheated oven for 10-15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the filling is heated through.
Remove the wrap from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Freeze for up to 2 months and thaw at room temperature before serving.
Reheat in the oven or microwave before serving.
